name: MediaProjection API
platform: Android platform API; introduced in API 21; Android 14+ adds stricter foreground-service and single-use rules
license: [uncertain] Android platform API; AOSP source is Apache 2.0, but the runtime API is distributed as part of Android
version: [uncertain] Current Android platform API (docs current as of 2026)
project_status: [uncertain] Actively maintained in the Android platform; MediaProjection and related Android 14/15 guidance are current in 2026
capture_driver: User-granted screen or app-window capture via MediaProjection; long screenshots require app-controlled scrolling plus external frame collection and stitching
compose_support: Indirect only. It can capture visible Compose pixels, but it does not natively capture full LazyColumn or verticalScroll content
view_support: Any visible on-screen UI: View, RecyclerView, WebView, Compose, and other rendered content subject to platform/privacy limits
stitching_strategy: Not built in. Long screenshots require app-defined vertical stitching over multiple captured frames
matching_algorithm: None in the API itself; any matching algorithm is application-defined
scroll_offset_trust: Not provided by the API; apps must infer scroll progress from their own UI logic or pixel analysis
overlap_requirement: No platform requirement; app-defined if stitching is used
confidence_fallback: No platform fallback; the app must implement retries, overlap checks, or abandon stitching
static_chrome_handling: Full-display capture includes app chrome and can include system bars; Android 14 app-window capture excludes status bar, notifications, and other system UI outside the chosen app window
dynamic_content_robustness: Captures live pixels, so animations, video, timestamps, and lazy loading can drift between frames during long-screenshot workflows
content_type_fitness: Good as a generic visible-screen capture primitive; poor as a standalone long-content solution without custom scrolling and stitching
memory_strategy: Streams frames through a VirtualDisplay/Surface pipeline; any large-bitmap assembly is app-defined
stitching_speed: App-defined; the API only supplies frames
max_output_constraints: [uncertain] Bounded by virtual-display size, Surface/ImageReader throughput, bitmap memory, and any app-side export limits
output_format: Raw frames to Surface/ImageReader; final file format is app-defined
output_quality: Near-source quality for captured pixels; final compression depends on the app
oem_compatibility: [uncertain] OEM privacy rules, protected-content behavior, background restrictions, and screenshot policies can vary
integration: android.media.projection.MediaProjectionManager + foreground service + VirtualDisplay/Surface pipeline; no Jetpack dependency required
media_projection_compliance: Required. Targeting Android 14+ needs a foreground service of type mediaProjection, FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION permission, user consent before starting capture, and startForeground() with the mediaProjection type; newer Android versions also enforce stricter single-use/token rules and Android 15+ restricts launching this FGS from BOOT_COMPLETED
windowing_behavior: Full-display capture from API 21; Android 14 app-screen-sharing can capture a single app window; behavior varies with split-screen, rotation, and foldables
data_path: Fully on-device unless the app uploads captured frames
permissions_required: User consent via createScreenCaptureIntent; FOREGROUND_SERVICE and FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION on Android 14+; optional additional permissions only for extra media features such as audio capture
ai_ml_capability: [uncertain] None in the platform API; any ML would be app-side
server_side_pipeline: Optional app design only; not part of the API
chart_stitching_fitness: [uncertain] Moderate only when paired with strong app-side stitching; the API alone does not solve long-chart continuity
chat_stitching_fitness: [uncertain] Moderate only when paired with strong app-side stitching; repeated avatars and sticky headers can complicate external stitching
